title: Garlicky Gooey Japanese Oats Stir-fry
description: A fragrant, tofurich stirfry where quick oats soak up a garlicky soymiso sauce, creating a gooey, comforting dish perfect for a romantic Japanese lunch.

ingredients.name: sesame oil
ingredients.notes: divided: 1 tbsp for tofu, 1 tbsp for stir-fry

instructions: Rinse the oats lightly with cold water and set aside; they’ll absorb the sauce later.
Press the tofu with paper towels to remove excess moisture, then cut into 1‑inch cubes.
Whisk together soy sauce, miso paste, minced garlic, and a pinch of red pepper flakes (optional) to create the base sauce.
Heat a non‑stick skillet or wok over medium‑high heat and add 1/2 tbsp sesame oil; when shimmering, add tofu cubes and sear until golden on all sides.
Remove tofu and set aside; in the same pan, add sliced carrots, shiitake mushrooms, and spinach; stir‑fry for 3–4 minutes until the veggies soften.
Sprinkle oats evenly over the vegetables, pour the sauce, and stir vigorously to coat everything; cook for another 5 minutes, allowing the oats to become silky and slightly gooey.
Return tofu to the pan and toss to combine; finish with chopped scallions, nori flakes, and toasted sesame seeds.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra soy sauce or a squeeze of lime if desired.
Transfer to serving plates, garnish with additional sesame seeds, and serve immediately while warm.
